caption.full:
John Leguizamo wore blind contacts for 14 hours daily on The Odyssey.

John Leguizamo revealed he wore contact lenses that rendered him effectively blind for 12 to 14 hours a day while filming Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey. He described the experience as so intense, requiring assistance for basic needs like eating and using the bathroom.

Was this level of commitment worth the risk, or did it cross a line?

AI-assisted recap by The Screen Report.

#MovieNews #Hollywood #JohnLeguizamo #TheOdyssey
caption.firstComment: Method acting shouldn’t endanger basic sight and safety—talent stands without self-destruction.

facebook.text: John Leguizamo wore blind contact lenses for 12-14 hours daily while filming Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey. He stated, "Not being able to see for 12 to 14 hours a day, like I couldn’t see my phone, I couldn’t read, I couldn’t call." Leguizamo had to be led to the bathroom and meals during filming. What do you think of this intense acting commitment? Share your thoughts below!

youtube.title: John Leguizamo Blind for 14 Hours Daily in The Odyssey
youtube.description:
John Leguizamo wore contact lenses that rendered him effectively blind for 12-14 hours daily while filming Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey. He stated, 'Your body doesn't know the difference' regarding the intense emotional toll. Matt Damon stars as Odysseus in the adaptation.
